name: Start Local Server and Create First Workflow
query: Start a local Conductor server and create a simple hello world workflow
expected_behavior: Step 1: Check if `conductor` CLI is installed, install if missing
Step 2: Start local server using `conductor server start`
Step 3: Verify server is running with `conductor server status`
Step 4: Test connectivity with `conductor workflow list` (should return empty list)
Step 5: No auth setup needed for local server (no 401/403)
Step 6: Consult references/workflow-definition.md for JSON schema
Step 7: Create a simple workflow definition JSON and write to file
Step 8: Register with `conductor workflow create workflow.json`
Step 9: Start the workflow with `conductor workflow start -w <name> -i '{}'`
Step 10: Check execution status and report result
success_criteria: Local server is started before attempting any API calls
Server status is verified before proceeding
No auth credentials are set or requested for local server
Workflow definition is valid JSON written to a file first
Workflow is registered before being started
Execution status is checked and reported to user
Server start uses the CLI, not Docker or manual steps
